title: What if we did our own dinky summer gig — Mark Cuban smart-homes pivot
html:
TL;DR

  • Late-May 2022 self-pitch: instead of a regular summer job, run a "dinky gig" — "could set my own hours, don't have a ceiling, but also have a larger floor."
  • Brainstormed gig list: gutter cleaning, door-to-door address stenciling, freelance marketing, boosting business online presence, and "Setting up smart homes — MARK CUBAN STYLE!!!!"
  • Earliest extant articulation of the floor-vs-ceiling trade Yahir runs again and again: take optionality + uncapped upside, accept higher variance.
  • The "mark cuban style smart-home install" line is a clean datapoint on Yahir's 2022 cohort — Cuban as practical-business-template, not just internet figure.
  • Door-to-door address stenciling is the first appearance of the door to door hustle thread that recurs in 2024–25 lawn-care / Brotein direct-sales daydreams.

Key line

"could set my own hours, don't have a ceiling, but also have a larger floor"

Why it matters

This is the founder-seed: not a startup, not a product — just the gig-vs-job optionality trade Yahir runs forever after. The same logic re-appears in:

  • 2022 05 24 c not python cs161 "apply for more jobs , maybe not"
  • 2022 10 25 ahmad jamal stream PDX agency hit-list (apply to many vs run own thing)
  • the Brotein arc (gym-job vs build-the-app)
  • the Austin founder grind (W-2 vs runway)

The Mark Cuban smart-homes line is also worth watching — Cuban shows up again in the 2023+ business-template references.

Open questions

  • Did Yahir actually do any of these gigs in summer 2022? (Best guess: no — he ended up in Vietnam instead.)
  • "Smart homes Mark Cuban style" — what specific Cuban content was Yahir consuming? (Shark Tank? AXS TV?)
  • Was the "we" in "we did our own dinky gig" aspirational, or was a specific co-founder in mind? (No name attached.)

Wikilinks
